report_name: Ransomware Market Consolidates in Q1 2026; Qilin Remains Top Threat as LockBit 5.0 Rebounds

threat_actors.name: Qilin
threat_actors.description: The dominant ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) group in Q1 2026, leading the market for the third consecutive quarter with 338 publicly claimed victims.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration.

threat_actors.name: The Gentlemen
threat_actors.description: A Russian-speaking ransomware group that surged in activity in Q1 2026, securing the number three market position. The group notably focuses on non-Western targets in regions like APAC and Latin America, possibly by exploiting vulnerable FortiGate devices.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration.

threat_actors.name: LockBit
threat_actors.description: A major RaaS operation that made a significant comeback in Q1 2026 with its LockBit 5.0 variant after being disrupted by the 'Operation Cronos' law enforcement action. The group posted 163 victims, demonstrating the resilience of its brand and infrastructure.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data exfiltration.

threat_actors.name: SafePay
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group whose activity dropped by 77% in Q1 2026 after its data leak site went offline.

threat_actors.name: Devman
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group whose operations declined by 70% in Q1 2026 after its operator was placed on an Interpol wanted list.

threat_actors.name: Sinobi
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group whose public victim postings collapsed by 42% in Q1 2026.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The core activity of ransomware groups like Qilin and LockBit, involving the encryption of victim files for extortion.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: Used as part of a double-extortion tactic where sensitive data is stolen and posted to a leak site prior to file encryption to pressure victims into paying.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: A primary initial access vector for top-tier ransomware groups, who target vulnerabilities in internet-facing infrastructure such as FortiGate VPNs.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Desktop Protocol
attack_patterns.description: A common initial access method used by ransomware groups, often facilitated by initial access brokers selling RDP access.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware groups disable system recovery features, such as deleting volume shadow copies, to prevent victims from restoring their files without paying the ransom.

malware.name: Qilin
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by the Qilin group, which was the most active RaaS operation in Q1 2026.

malware.name: LockBit 5.0
malware.description: The new version of the LockBit ransomware that saw a major resurgence in Q1 2026, with a 106% increase in victims following law enforcement disruption.

infrastructure.name: Ransomware Data Leak Sites
infrastructure.description: Public-facing data leak sites used by ransomware groups like Qilin and LockBit to post victim data as part of their double-extortion strategy.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b50a0f4-8195-4059-8a37-16893d562d9e
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware precursor technique to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://research.checkpoint.com/2026/05/the-state-of-ransomware-q1-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-12
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware precursor technique to inhibit system recovery.
